description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance from above. The sides of the block are solid and smooth, contrasting with the textured top. The coloration is primarily a mix of dark gray and black, with the grooves appearing darker due to shadowing. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its overall form and finish indicate durability and resistance to weathering.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped, ribbed appearance. The sides are smooth and solid, and the coloration is a mix of dark gray and black.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side angle highlighting the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the block's thickness visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped top and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side angle, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the block's robust thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a standalone barrier
